title: Lycée Louis-le-Grand
highlights:
LLG, is a public Lycée(French secondary school, also known as sixth form college) located on rue Saint-Jacques in central Paris. It was founded in the early 1560s by the Jesuits as the Collège de Clermont, was renamed in 1682 after King Louis XIV("Louis the Great"), and has remained at the apex of France's secondary education system despite  ...    Collegium Ludovici Magni (French: Collège Louis-le-Grand). That act confirmed its royal patronage, despite  ...    ## Notable alumni  Louis-le-Grand has long been considered to play an important role in the education of French elites. In 1762, just before the college's nationalization, scholar Jean-Baptiste-Jacques Élie de Beaumont wrote: "The Jesuit College of Paris has for a long time been a state nursery, the most fertile in great men."
